textA: Hello is anyone on zoladex injection for hormone positive breast cancer? What are the side effects?
textB: I have been on Zoladex for 5 years. It's like induced menopause. Hot flashes, mood swings, joint pain, weight gain.
label: attach
notes: direct answer to same treatment question

textA: After my mom's chemotherapy she is experiencing nausea weakness and hair loss. Any advice?
textB: Dear friend, many people experience nausea and weakness after chemo. Make sure she stays hydrated and eats protein rich food.
label: attach
notes: caregiver Q → specific advice response, same topic

textA: Good morning all I'm on hormone therapy but having excessive sweating especially on face. Anyone else?
textB: Good morning. Excessive sweating especially on the face and neck is a very common side effect of hormone therapy for breast cancer.
label: attach
notes: symptom Q → informational answer, strong overlap

textA: Is anyone on tamoxifen? I have terrible joint pain. My oncologist says it's normal but it's affecting my daily life.
textB: Yes I have the same issue. My doctor suggested vitamin D and light walking every day. It helped me a lot.
label: attach
notes: drug-side-effect Q → me-too + advice, same drug

textA: My oncologist started me on letrozole after 5 years of tamoxifen. Has anyone made this switch?
textB: I switched from tamoxifen to letrozole 2 years ago. The joint pain got a bit worse at first but improved. Hot flashes continued.
label: attach
notes: treatment switch Q → direct experience answer

textA: I am on chemo and experiencing severe hair loss. Will it grow back?
textB: Hair loss from chemo is almost always temporary. Most people see regrowth 3-6 months after treatment ends. It may come back curly at first.
label: attach
notes: chemo side effect Q → reassuring factual answer

textB: When is the next group meetup? I am in Pune.

textA: My CA125 levels went from 450 to 12 after 6 cycles. So happy.

textA: I am experiencing bone pain after letrozole. Is this normal?
textB: Yes, bone and joint pain are very common side effects of aromatase inhibitors like letrozole and anastrozole. Weight bearing exercise helps.
label: attach
notes: AI side effect Q → direct informational answer

textA: My oncologist recommended BRCA testing. Has anyone done this? What should I expect?
textB: I had BRCA1 positive result. It changed my treatment plan. The test itself is just a blood draw. Results take 2-3 weeks.
label: attach
notes: BRCA Q → first-hand experience answer, same topic but larger time gap

textA: Has anyone tried acupuncture for chemo nausea?
textB: I found ginger tea and small frequent meals much more helpful than acupuncture for nausea. Anti-nausea medication also helps.
label: attach
notes: alternative therapy Q → comparison answer on same symptom

textA: Radiation therapy ke baad skin bahut red ho gayi. Koi cream suggest karo.
textB: Aloe vera gel laga sakte hain. Jo cream radiation department wale dete hain woh best hai. Dhoop mein mat jao.

notes: hinglish radiation skin Q → hinglish remedy reply

textA: Zoladex ke baad hot flashes bahut zyada hain. Kuch help karega?
textB: Mujhe bhi ye problem thi. Doctor ne vitamin E suggest kiya tha. Cooling spray bhi useful hai.

textA: My port is causing infection. The site is red and swollen.
textB: You should contact your oncology nurse immediately. Port infections can become serious quickly. Don't wait for the next appointment.

textA: My platelets dropped to 40000 after last chemo cycle. Doctor delayed next cycle.
textB: Low platelets are common after chemo. Avoid NSAIDs, be careful about cuts, and rest. Usually recovers in a week or two.

notes: lab result concern → specific medical advice on same issue

textA: Has anyone had a recurrence after being NED for 3 years? How did you cope?
textB: I had recurrence after 2 years. It was devastating at first. But I focused on the new treatment plan and took it one day at a time.
